title: Can't enter containers permission denied: OCI permission denied
body:
Fedora 35
I tried first with the fedora 35 repos version  podman 3.4.4-1 and then with  podman 4.0.0-10.fc35.x86_64

`Error: unable to start container "d03e19bcfad2991a9bd4fd5069d8c2148e964f547a1f6c87e01f1c29832448f3": runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: error preparing rootfs: mount /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f28d4e438e68177945fc2750a8ec7dfb2527ee7b68d9aed866ac476b7df5b5f1/merged:/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f28d4e438e68177945fc2750a8ec7dfb2527ee7b68d9aed866ac476b7df5b5f1/merged, flags: 0x5000: permission denied: OCI permission denied`

I also tried this as user
```
~  podman run --rm -ti --ipc host --network host --privileged --security-opt label=disable --user root:root --pid host --userns keep-id --ulimit host --annotation run.oci.keep_original_groups=1 --mount type=devpts,destination=/dev/pts docker.io/archlinux:latest bash
Trying to pull docker.io/library/archlinux:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9ae2c166397c done
Copying blob baca2b2a1f03 done
Copying config 80d141e567 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: error preparing rootfs: mount /home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f4aaf8174628f76592d7574d496594a9406b86dd7f98e6f5ee1aec5252442d12/merged:/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f4aaf8174628f76592d7574d496594a9406b86dd7f98e6f5ee1aec5252442d12/merged, flags: 0x5000: permission denied: OCI permission denied 
```

comments.body: Please provide the full output of `podman info`


If you install `crun` and use `--runtime crun` does it work?

Seems like it is working. Anyway to make crun the default without having to specify it every time?
```
~  podman run --runtime crun -it  docker.io/archlinux:latest bash
[root@c57795cdc87c /]#
```
You can do this in containers.conf
Could you check if SELinux AVCs were generated?
how do i check that?

Make it fail again then

sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent

➜ @fedora  ~  podman run --rm -ti --ipc host --network host --privileged --security-opt label=disable --user root:root --pid host --userns keep-id --ulimit host --annotation run.oci.keep_original_groups=1 --mount type=devpts,destination=/dev/pts docker.io/archlinux:latest bash
Error: crun: make `/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/e13933a3f8ed141358e00e79ab18b975c3e02c2751aa55967dcf1c04a0780f46/merged` private: Permission denied: OCI permission denied                                                                                                                    /0.3s
➜ @fedora  ~  sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent
no matches

I did a "cp /usr/share/containers/containers.conf $HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf" but I am not sure on what to modify
http://sprunge.us/ShRu1D

```
# Default OCI runtime
#
#runtime = "crun"
```
Uncomment the runtime line.

This is ignored by runc ` --annotation run.oci.keep_original_groups=1`
Also it is easier to write --group-add keep-groups

podman run -it docker.io/archlinux:latest bash  is working now.

 podman run --rm -ti --ipc host --network host --privileged --security-opt label=disable --user root:root --pid host --userns keep-id --ulimit host --mount type=devpts,destination=/dev/pts docker.io/archlinux:latest bash
Error: crun: make `/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/89d157c9307189f6162a94d5589d8d87ce3604aa5b2042632f68ce3fe2022c8a/merged` private: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
can you share the content of `/proc/self/mountinfo`?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we got no more feedback, closing.  Reopen if you still want us to work on this issue.

@rhatdan It is reproducible in Podman 4.1 on Fedora Silverblue 36 WS. It doesn't look as SeLinux problem because switching SELinux off  doesn't solve it. But the real root cause may be in the mechanism of WS tempfiles creatin during session login. I see only root level configuration for systemd-tmpfiles... unit under /etc that manages temp files under /tmp but expect also user-level configuration for tmp-setup running unit running on login. Sorry if I'm wrong.
Not sure what Podman could do to fix this?

@rhatdan systemd-tmpfiles.conf in /usr/lib/tmpfiles.d used by root contains some lines that protect the Podman's storage in the root mode from been touched by tmpfiles daemon. 
Line 6 in the podman.conf in  podman.conf. 
But tmpfiles unit runs also on every Session login as I see. If user-level tmpfiles configuration for podman is missed mean s that any part of disk space used by Podman in the rootless mode can be managed by tmpfiles daemon randomly, It is good for disk space management per user and security but [tmpfiles conf doc](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/tmpfiles.d.html) is not enough detailed to understand how tmpfiles rules are applied. If mode can be changed on-the-fly "access denied" can happen without any SELinux involvement. Indeed, switching seinforce changes nothing.  
But using Logout/Reboot/Login  I easily got access denied on the my own /run/user/1000 ????? Something went wrong with mode  😒

Hm, Podman Podman runtime directory always contains btrfs-locks lock file. Is it OK? When I switch to another user it doesn't disappear and keeps the same owner that created it. It looks strange. I would expect that the lock is cleaned up by tmpfiles daemon because its owner gnome-terminal process is certainly dead.

This issue looks like hangs but it prevents podman from using in Fedora 36. Just another observation: the AVC error tells that the systemd-journald attempts to access sys_ptrace and blocked. On other hand podman passes to conmon 2 options according to debug log: ol journald and --syslog. When the 1st one results in regular journald log recording the conmon documentation says that the last one will result in writing dumps to syslog but it is impossible userns due to lack of required cap. This is more or less SETroubleshut says me.
Please show me the AVCs?

Raw Audit Messages
type=AVC msg=audit(1652897010.86:799): avc:  denied  { sys_ptrace } for  pid=590 comm="systemd-journal" capability=19  scontext=system_u:system_r:syslogd_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:syslogd_t:s0 tclass=cap_userns permissive=0


@rhatdan 
This is what SELinux troubleshooting shows:

P.S. I looked into systemd core dump daemon and reinstalled/rechecked if it is running on my machine. All Kernel, Core dump config files, unit files and activated socket are in the correct place. Maybe, the direct write into core dump files by rootless users is not expected anymore.

Hash: systemd-journal,syslogd_t,syslogd_t,cap_userns,sys_ptrace

And more: although capsh --print

gid=1000(pavelsosin)
groups=10(wheel),1000(pavelsosin)
Guessed mode: UNCERTAIN (0)
crun and conmon have no caps. If all necessary caps are propagated to the OCI runtime?

Even redirection  of output > AVCError.txt in my homedir causes Permission denied error. 
Doe SELinux work at all on dynamically mounted filesystems like tmp and home?

It is not necessary to dig too deep. : wrong parameter is passed to conmon. Podman passes --syslog
_DEBU[0000] /usr/bin/conmon **messages will be logged to syslog** 
DEBU[0000] running conmon: /usr/bin/conmon               args="[--api-version 1 -c 1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08 -u 1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08 -r /usr/bin/crun -b /home/pavelsosin/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs-containers/1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08/userdata -p /run/user/1000/containers/storage/btrfs-containers/1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08/userdata/pidfile -n elated_johnson --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --full-attach -s -l journald -**-log-level debug --syslog --conmon-pidfile** /run/user/1000/containers/storage/btrfs-containers/1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/pavelsosin/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg debug --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --network-config-dir --exit-command-arg  --exit-command-arg --network-backend --exit-command-arg cni --exit-command-arg --volumepath --exit-command-arg /home/pavelsosin/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg btrfs --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg --syslog --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08]"
INFO[0000] Running conmon under slice user.slice and unitName libpod-conmon-1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08.scope 
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied

DEBU[0000] Received: -1                                 
DEBU[0000] Cleaning up container 1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08 
DEBU[0000] Tearing down network namespace at /run/user/1000/netns/netns-13872b40-cd2d-07e7-6ec7-b9c87a2b9aa0 for container 1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08 
DEBU[0000] Unmounted container "1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08" 
Error: unable to start container "1ae55650cee30350731332132760b1e772b071127d143b79dfed57e517b2fa08": crun: [conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied_

open executable: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

when conmon cleary states in its help --syslog                     Log to syslog (use with cgroupfs cgroup manager)
If systemd is already in use then using journald and coredumpd sockets  is assumed to **keep podman secure**.
conmon running in the user space has very few chances to write into syslog due to lack of required capability.

Even in the case when log-driver=journald is passed explicitly to the podman container create by the rootless user podman start of the created container debug log shows that --syslog option is passed to the conmon and conmon says "conmon messages will be logged to syslog". It's expected that conmon writes messages only to designated target like journald, systemd sd using designated driver.

@InvisibleRasta Are you satisfied with this issue status? Please, reopen or send me mail directly to create a new issue. Podman still doesn't work on Fedora 36 modular with Kernel 5.17 after upgrade when podman start command is executed from the gnome-terminal as rootless user. No SeLinux events are registered. Podman info shows that syslog capability is missed when conmon explicitly says that the target of the log is syslog ignoring configuration and passed option. capsh --print from the gnome-terminal shows  that the bash process has syslog capabilities.
It looks like podman,conmon,crun capability  set is too narrow to perform some operations.
